data_in.description: mzML files (centroided) corresponding to LC-MS runs for targeted analysis
Target list data frame with columns: compound ID, compound name, m/z, expected retention time (minutes), and polarity
Sample metadata including type labels (e.g., QC, sample) for each mzML file

data_out.description: EIC PNG diagnostic figures saved to output folder for visual inspection of target peak detection in screening mode
Verification report confirming EIC PNG files from MsExperiment-based invocation match file-path-based reference invocation

domain_knowledge: MsExperiment is a container class that combines Spectra objects with sample-level metadata, enabling integrated downstream analysis without separate file-path indexing.
Screening mode in tardisPeaks() checks whether targets are detectable within their defined m/z and retention-time windows before performing full peak integration across all samples.

EIC (Extracted Ion Chromatogram) PNG files are diagnostic outputs that visualize the actual chromatographic signal for each target, enabling manual quality assessment and troubleshooting of detection parameters.
Centroiding converts raw profile-mode mass spectrometry data to centroid-mode by reducing each peak cluster to a single m/z–intensity pair, which is required for TARDIS input files in mzML format.

API equivalence testing verifies that two functionally identical code paths (object-based vs. file-path-based invocation) produce identical or visually indistinguishable results, confirming correct wrapper implementation.

direct_checks: verify that vignette mzML files exist in the package distribution
verify that tardisPeaks() function accepts both MsExperiment object and file-path inputs
verify that screening_mode parameter is accepted by tardisPeaks() function
verify that output directory contains Diagnostic EIC PNG files after execution
file_format_is: output files are PNG format
verify that PNG files generated from MsExperiment object invocation exist in expected output directory
verify that PNG files generated from file-path-based invocation exist in expected output directory
robust comparison of PNG file dimensions, byte-for-byte matching not required due to potential compression variations
expert_review: visual inspection and quantitative comparison of EIC chromatograms from MsExperiment-based vs. file-path-based invocations for equivalence in peak detection patterns, retention time accuracy, and ion intensity representation
assessment of whether minor rendering differences (e.g., color profiles, font rendering, metadata) constitute meaningful divergence from expected reproducibility

cmd: library(xcms); library(Spectra); library(MsExperiment); library(TARDIS); mzml_files <- list.files('{inputs.mzml_files}', pattern='\.mzML$', full.names=TRUE); spc <- Spectra(mzml_files); metadata <- read.csv('{inputs.sample_metadata}'); rownames(metadata) <- basename(mzml_files); mse <- MsExperiment(spectra=spc, sampleData=metadata); targets <- read.csv('{inputs.target_list}'); result <- tardisPeaks(lcmsData=mse, targets=targets, screening_mode=TRUE, output_dir='output'); cat('Task completed: EIC PNGs generated and comparison performed\n')
executable.grounding_notes: Unverified by the executable guard — command runs inline code (-c/-e); its API calls cannot be validated against package files. Treat this command as a best-effort inference; verify the entry point and APIs against the real code before running.

finding: The tardisPeaks() function accepts both file paths and MsExperiment objects as input when screening_mode=TRUE, with the MsExperiment approach requiring sampleData$type to be populated to distinguish QC from sample runs.
inputs: mzML files (centroided) corresponding to LC-MS runs for targeted analysis

methodology_summary: Load vignette mzML files into a Spectra object via the Spectra package.
Prepare sample metadata with type labels and combine with Spectra to construct an MsExperiment object.
Define a target list with compound identifiers, chemical names, m/z values, retention times, and ion polarity.
Execute tardisPeaks() in screening mode (screening_mode=TRUE) using the MsExperiment object as lcmsData to check target visibility within expected m/z and RT windows.
Extract and compare diagnostic EIC PNG files from MsExperiment-based invocation against reference EICs from file-path-based invocation to confirm functional equivalence.
Validation: EIC PNG files produced by both invocation methods are identical in file content and visual representation, confirming correct MsExperiment integration.

task_description: Construct an MsExperiment object from vignette mzML files with sample type labels, execute tardisPeaks() in screening mode using the MsExperiment object as lcmsData input, and verify that the resulting diagnostic EIC PNG files match those from file-path-based invocation.

Target compound list with ID, name, m/z, RT, and polarity

task_objective: Validate that MsExperiment-based and file-path-based tardisPeaks() invocations produce identical screening-mode EIC PNG outputs.

title: Extend TARDIS to accept an MsExperiment object as input and reproduce the screening-mode EIC plots
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Load mzML files into a Spectra object using the Spectra package. 2. Create a sampleData data frame with type labels (e.g., QC, sample) for each mzML file. 3. Construct an MsExperiment object combining the Spectra object and sampleData. 4. Prepare a target list data frame containing compound ID, name, theoretical m/z, expected retention time in minutes, and polarity annotation. 5. Execute tardisPeaks() with the MsExperiment object as the lcmsData argument, screening_mode=TRUE to check target visibility within m/z and RT windows, and specify the output folder path. 6. Extract diagnostic EIC PNG files from the output folder generated by the MsExperiment-based invocation. 7. Compare the EIC PNG file set and visual content against reference EIC PNGs produced from the equivalent file-path-based tardisPeaks() invocation to verify functional equivalence.
